titel: Next steps for good and accessible healthcare.
html:
The proposal suggests changes to the Dental Care Act (1985:125), the Patient Act (2014:821), and the Health and Medical Care Act (2017:30). The proposals primarily aim to clarify the responsibilities of primary care and to strengthen access to medical expertise in municipal health and medical care. The proposals include the following:

  • It is clarified that regions and municipalities must collaborate in the planning and development of health and medical care.
  • It is clarified that primary care must address both physical and mental health needs.
  • A requirement is introduced for a medically responsible person for rehabilitation in the municipality.
  • It is clarified that a medical assessment by a doctor and nurse should be offered, if necessary, regardless of the time of day in municipal health and medical care.
  • The term "home care" is removed and replaced, where appropriate, with "health and medical care at home."
  • The information requirements for patients are strengthened. A patient should receive information about who their primary care contact and primary physician are, and how the patient can contact their primary care contact, primary physician, and healthcare facilities.

The proposed amendments are scheduled to come into effect on July 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

The government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Dental Care Act (1985:125).
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Patient Act (2014:821).
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Health and Medical Care Act (2017:30).
tldr: The government wants to change the laws to improve and clarify healthcare. The goal is to make it easier for you to receive the right care and to know who is responsible for your treatment.
lattlast:
The government has proposed changes to three key laws governing dental and healthcare services. If the parliament approves the proposal, the new regulations will come into effect on July 1, 2026. The proposal aims to clarify the responsibilities of healthcare centers and how municipalities and regions should collaborate.

A crucial aspect is to improve the quality of care when you live at home. It will become a requirement that there is a doctor or nurse available to make medical assessments when needed, regardless of the time of day. The goal is also to ensure that healthcare centers provide support for both physical injuries and mental health issues.

It will also become easier for you, as a patient, to understand your own care. You will receive clear information about who your primary care physician is and who your designated contact person is within the healthcare system. You will also be informed about how to contact these individuals or departments.

Finally, the language in the laws will be revised to be more accurate. The term "home healthcare" will be replaced with "healthcare at home." The purpose of all these changes is to create a more cohesive and secure healthcare system for all residents.
